commit.message:
Implement theme system (Light / Dark / System default)

Wires the existing Settings > Theme dropdown to actual visual changes:
- Four CoffeeSettings colour accessors (ThemePanelBg, ThemeTextColor,
  ThemeDimTextColor, ThemeOutlineColor) return palette values per theme
- SyncAllWindows() broadcasts MSG_THEME_CHANGED to all open windows
- Custom Draw() methods (ParticleGaugeView, SieveDistView, CalHistView,
  ExtractionBarView, RoastGaugeView, ThumbView) use theme colours for
  backgrounds, outlines, tick marks, and labels
- BTextView tips areas update view/text colour on MSG_THEME_CHANGED and
  in constructors so newly opened windows inherit the active theme
- Coffee-colour gradients and extraction zone colours are unchanged

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add PNG icon build step and update documentation

commit.message:
Add context tokens field to Local mode

Adds a persistent "Context tokens" field in the Local Settings box
(below the Model field, default 32768). At launch, the value is
injected as OLLAMA_NUM_CTX and LM_STUDIO_NUM_CTX so both Ollama and
LM Studio use the configured context window. Values below 32768 are
clamped. The setting is saved and restored across sessions.

fix: rename GlobalError→RouteError in error.tsx + suppress S6759

- app/error.tsx: rename export to RouteError to avoid duplicate name
  with global-error.tsx (both previously exported GlobalError)
- sonar-project.properties: suppress typescript:S6759 on frontend/**
  (error boundary prop destructuring is required by Next.js contract)

fix: add --no-install-recommends to apt-get install (docker:S6500)

Reduces image attack surface by not installing unnecessary recommended
packages alongside gcc and libpq-dev in the backend Docker image.

merge: integrate main (CAE sub-tabs + Railway overhaul) into sanarQubIssuesFIxes

- backend/Dockerfile: combined non-root user security fix with start.sh entrypoint,
  merged consecutive RUN instructions into one layer

  our alert() fix was already absorbed

ci: add type-check script and suppress S1128 for app pages

- Add `type-check` npm script (tsc --noEmit) to frontend/package.json
  to catch type-import regressions like the Phase 8 RequiredFieldsForm/CalendarView incident
- Suppress typescript:S1128 (unused imports) on frontend/app/** in sonar-project.properties
  to avoid false positives on JSX component imports that SonarCloud misidentifies as type-only

ping_agent: remove status updates, delegate to auth_agent

Ping agent now only records metrics and updates last_ping_at.
Status (online/offline) is managed exclusively by auth_agent.

fix: update Zig download URL pattern and bump to 0.15.2

Zig 0.15.x changed archive naming from zig-linux-{arch} to
zig-{arch}-linux. Updated Dockerfile.base URL pattern and bumped
version to latest (0.15.2). Regenerated all bindings.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(sprint-4): payments, planning, dashboard + review fixes

Sprint 4 — nouvelles fonctionnalités :
- Modèle Payment (Prisma) + migration add-payments
- API paiements : /api/orders/[id]/payments, /api/payments, /api/payments/[id]/receipt
- API facture PDF : /api/orders/[id]/invoice
- API stats dashboard : /api/dashboard/stats (param period)
- Composants : PaymentSummary, PaymentForm, PaymentHistory
- Pages : /dashboard/payments, /dashboard/calendar
- Dashboard : KPIs réels, commandes récentes, échéances 7j

Revue complète sprints 1-4 — corrections :
- fix(monetary): totalPaid stocké en FCFA (cohérent avec totalPrice), conversion centimes→FCFA lors agrégation
- fix(orders): auto-création Payment(ADVANCE) à la création commande si avance > 0
- fix(dashboard): skeleton loaders remplacent les tirets, filtre période CA
- fix(order-form): Select shadcn pour type vêtement, recherche client, reste à payer dynamique
- fix(client-form): centrage mx-auto
- fix(measurements): champs libres par client (ajout/suppression sans contrainte de template)
- fix(navigation): lien Mesures dans sidebar
- fix(planning): description du principe ajoutée
- fix(neon): connect_timeout=15 dans DATABASE_URL pour éviter P1001

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(build): remove JSX.Element return types to fix Vercel TS build

JSX namespace isn't available without explicit React import in this
tsconfig setup. Dropping the redundant explicit return types lets TS
infer them, unblocking the Vercel production build.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(build): exclude test files from tsc -b and restore JSX types

- Add src/vite-env.d.ts (standard Vite pattern) so vite/client types
  are available without overriding TypeScript's "types" field
- Remove "types": ["vite/client"] from tsconfig.app.json — this was
  blocking @types/react auto-discovery, causing JSX.Element errors
- Exclude *.test.ts/tsx from tsconfig.app.json so production build
  does not compile Vitest globals (vi, describe, expect)

commit.message:
Unify save/reflect/sync into single polling cycle

- Edit stays in memory only (dirty flag), no file I/O on keystroke
- Every 5s poll: flush to JSON → snapshot diff → auto-reflect to Excel + log → sync remote changes
- Remove Reflect button/menu (automatic now)
- Remove Save-SourceData/Save-CurrentRecord (replaced by Flush-EditorToMemory + Save-AllDirtyToJson)
- Changelog only written when diffs are actually committed to Excel

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

research: CRT ring isomorphism, Borsuk-Ulam fixes, knowledge updates (#3398)

* research: extend yang-mills with OS axioms, beta function, spectral gap theory

- Part XXXIII: Osterwalder-Schrader framework (Euclidean QFT axioms, Schwinger functions,
  reflection positivity, time reflection involution, mass gap from exponential decay)
- Part XXXIV: Asymptotic freedom and beta function (β₀=11N/3, running coupling,
  asymptotic freedom theorem, Λ_QCD scale, trace anomaly)
- Part XXXV: Spectral gap and correlation length (ξ=1/Δ, Källén-Lehmann spectral
  representation, lattice correlation length, continuum limit growth)
- Also proves slowlyVarying_rpow in CLT (converts axiom → theorem)
- 268 defs/theorems, 0 sorries, 2866 lines total

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* research: update yang-mills knowledge (OS axioms, beta function, spectral gap)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* research: extend CLT with SV division, asymptotic equiv, RV inverse/quotient

- slowlyVarying_div: quotient of slowly varying functions is slowly varying
- slowlyVarying_of_asymp_equiv: asymptotic equivalence preserves slow variation
- slowlyVarying_pow: integer powers of slowly varying functions
- regularlyVarying_inv_index: reciprocal negates the index
- regularlyVarying_div_index: quotient subtracts indices
- 40 defs/theorems, 0 sorries, 667 lines

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* research: update CLT knowledge (SV/RV extensions)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* research: extend Fourier series with Fejér kernel, Bessel inequality, Parseval theorem

- Part XI: Fejér kernel and Cesàro summability (kernel def, continuity, Fejér's theorem)
- Part XII: Bessel inequality, Parseval theorem, Fourier uniqueness theorem
- Proved: Fourier uniqueness from Parseval (if all coefficients = 0 then ‖f‖₂ = 0)
- 45 defs/theorems (was 28), 2 sorries, 653 lines (was 442)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* research: update fourier-series knowledge (Fejér, Parseval)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* research: prove 3/4 sorries in BorsukUlamOQ03OQ03 (Tucker→BU bridge)

Proved approx_pair_small_diff (antisymmetricDiff ↔ dist),
approx_to_exact (compact S¹ minimization argument), and
borsuk_ulam_2d_from_tucker (chains approx + compactness).
1 sorry remains: explicit triangulation construction.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* research: formalize CRT ring isomorphism (bezout-identity-oq-03-oq-01)

New file BezoutIdentityOQ03OQ01.lean: 26 proved theorems, 0 sorries, 0 axioms.
Wraps Mathlib's ZMod.chineseRemainder with explicit properties:
- Ring isomorphism ℤ/mnℤ ≅ ℤ/mℤ × ℤ/nℤ (bijectivity, hom properties)
- Inverse map and round-trip identities
- Orthogonal idempotent decomposition (e₁+e₂=1, e₁e₂=0, eᵢ²=eᵢ)
- Bézout's identity connection (explicit CRT solution formula)
- Cardinality preservation and Euler totient multiplicativity
- Simultaneous congruence existence/uniqueness
- Power preservation and unit group characterization

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* research: update bezout-identity-oq-03-oq-01 knowledge (COMPLETED)

Improve rendering efficiency and add reliability improvements

- Track x/y counters in GridDiff instead of dividing per cell

- Add 27 unit tests across color, dither, grid, and renderer modules

commit.message:
Fix JSON parse error: escape typographic quotes in slide text

The closing smart quotes (U+201E/U+201D) in Estonian text were written
as plain ASCII quotes, breaking the JSON parser. Fixed all 7 occurrences.

fix: auto-approve cd prefix on safe bash commands

Commands like `cd ~/project && git add ... && git commit ...` were
falling through to Tier 3 because _is_safe_git_compound required
the command to start with `git add`. Strip leading `cd <path> &&`
before pattern matching since cd only sets working directory.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(steering): remove background watcher, use hook-only delivery

The background steering watcher (infinite while/sleep loop launched via
run_in_background) prevents claude from exiting after continue:false,
causing the process to hang after status-kill fires. The steering-relay
PostToolUse hook already delivers steering after every tool call, making
the background watcher redundant.

commit.message:
fix: use mermaid.render() API instead of innerHTML injection for v11 compat The root cause of 'Syntax error in text' in the Diagrams tab was that diagram Mermaid code was being set via innerHTML (template literal interpolation), which caused the browser to HTML-parse the content before Mermaid saw it. This corrupted
nodes into DOM elements and garbled label text. Changes: - diagrams.js _renderMermaid(): switch from mermaid.run() to mermaid.render(id, rawString) which passes the string directly to the Mermaid parser without browser HTML parsing - diagrams.js card HTML: remove mermaid code from innerHTML, render into empty div via mermaid.render() after DOM insertion - diagrams.js fullscreen: same fix - empty div + async mermaid.render() - app.js _renderMermaid(): switch from textContent + mermaid.run() to per-block async mermaid.render() calls for consistency and correct v11 behavior Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6

Add performance benchmarks and regression tests (#8) (#15)

- Add criterion benchmarks in benches/performance.rs (insert 10K chars,
  load 1M lines, cursor movement on 100K lines, get_visible_lines,
  insert_text_at_cursor)
- Add integration regression tests in tests/performance.rs with firm
  time limits to catch performance regressions
- Add lib.rs to expose public API for benchmarks and integration tests
- Add informational (non-blocking) bench job to CI workflow
- Configure criterion bench harness in Cargo.toml

commit.message:
fix(security): 修复3个安全漏洞

1. [高危] Timing Attack — admin secret 用 hmac.compare_digest 常数时间比对
   影响: admin.py / credits.py / feedback.py 三处 _require_admin
   原因: Python 'not in set' 非常数时间，可被计时攻击暴力破解

2. [中危] TOCTOU 竞态 — 邀请码并发注册多消耗名额
   影响: auth.py _consume_invite
   修复: 乐观锁 (.eq('use_count', current_value))，并发写入只有一个成功
         竞态触发时记录 warning 日志

3. [中危] 文件类型仅验证扩展名 — 无魔术字节校验
   影响: artifact_service.py store_file
   修复: 新增 _MAGIC_BYTES + _check_magic_bytes()
         PDF 需以 %PDF 开头，DOCX 需以 PK\x03\x04 开头
         伪造扩展名的文件直接 400 拒绝

两遍 175/175 全部通过

commit.message:
Restructure to production-grade GitOps architecture

- App-of-Apps with ArgoCD Projects (platform/workloads RBAC isolation)
- Helm wrapper charts with pinned versions for all platform services
- ApplicationSet for automatic workload discovery
- Sync waves: cert-manager/ingress(-3) → resources(-2) → monitoring/argocd(-1) → workloads(0)
- Resource requests/limits on all containers
- Health probes on all workloads
- ServiceMonitor integration for observability
- ServerSideApply for large CRD charts
- Retry policies with exponential backoff

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: handle multi-line YAML scalar values in SKILL.md frontmatter (#243)

## Summary

- Fix `parseMarkdownFrontmatter` to handle YAML block scalar style
(multi-line values with indented continuation lines) for all keys, not
just `metadata`
- This fixes skill discovery for repos like `vercel-labs/agent-skills`
where `composition-patterns` and `react-native-skills` use multi-line
`description` fields
- Add test coverage for multi-line scalar parsing in both
`loader.test.ts` and `remote.test.ts`

## Bug

The simple YAML parser in `parseMarkdownFrontmatter`
(`src/skills/loader.ts`) only collected indented continuation lines for
the `metadata` key. When `description` (or any other key) used YAML
block scalar style:

```yaml
description:
  React composition patterns that scale. Use when refactoring components with
  boolean prop proliferation, building flexible component libraries, or
  designing reusable APIs.
```

the parser silently skipped the value, leaving
`frontmatter['description']` unset. This caused `loadSkillFromMarkdown`
to throw `"Invalid markdown: missing 'description' in frontmatter"`,
which made `discoverRemoteSkills` skip these skills entirely (the error
is caught and the skill is silently ignored).

## Reproduction

1. Add `vercel-labs/agent-skills` as a remote in `warden.toml`
2. Run `warden add --remote vercel-labs/agent-skills
vercel-composition-patterns`
3. The skill is not found, even though
`skills/composition-patterns/SKILL.md` exists with a valid `name:
vercel-composition-patterns`

Affected skills: `composition-patterns` and `react-native-skills` (both
use multi-line `description`). `react-best-practices` and
`web-design-guidelines` work fine (single-line `description`).

## Fix

Track `inMultilineScalar` state (analogous to the existing `inMetadata`
state). When a key has no inline value and is not `metadata`, start
collecting indented continuation lines. When the next top-level key is
encountered (or the frontmatter ends), join the collected lines with
spaces and assign them to the key.

## Test plan

- [x] 3 new tests in `loader.test.ts` for multi-line description parsing
(block scalar, block scalar + metadata, single-line control)
- [x] 1 new test in `remote.test.ts` for remote skill discovery with
multi-line description
- [x] All 1298 existing tests pass (0 failures)
- [x] Verified against all 4 skills in `vercel-labs/agent-skills`: all
parse correctly after the fix

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: platform readiness gap-closing — DB migration, Colyseus schema, agent movement, auth, webhooks

Close all 5 platform readiness gaps:

- Phase 1: Initial Alembic migration (0000) creating departments, agents,
  approval_requests, swarm_tasks, compute_token_ledger tables
- Phase 2: Add currentTaskId, currentTaskDescription, departmentId to
  Colyseus AgentSchema; populate from API + Redis pub/sub in OfficeRoom
- Phase 3: AgentBehavior state machine (idle patrol, working freeze,
  waiting_approval walk-to-station, error stop) with movement in OfficeScene
- Phase 4: Authenticated API client (apiFetch), JWT session parsing
  (getSessionUser), Janua SSO login page, user identity in HUD
- Phase 5: GitHub webhook endpoint (POST /api/v1/gateway/github) with
  HMAC-SHA256 verification, PR/issue/CI-failure to SwarmTask mapping

Tests: 21 AgentBehavior, 11 api.ts, 10 gateway webhook, 5 agent-sync
schema tests added (total: 375 TS + 241 Python passing)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fire iOS haptics directly instead of deferring to dead click handler

The click handler never fired because touchstart's preventDefault()
suppresses synthetic click events. Now call _iosHapticPulse() directly
from hapticTap/Success/Error/Impact. Remove _pendingHaptic and the
unused click handler.

repository.description: Personal development environment configuration using Nix

commit.message:
Improve Discord verification flow, Business Finder UI, and phone extraction

Discord:
- Member dashboard: added step-by-step !verify instructions box
- Post-purchase email: added Discord invite button + detailed !verify steps with user's email
- Both explain how to message the bot to get Soldi Paid Member role

Business Finder:
- "Has Website" badge now blue and clickable (links to business website)
- Added "Website" column with Visit link for businesses that have sites
- Rows with websites less dimmed (0.7 opacity vs 0.5)
- Phone extraction Phase 2: scrapes actual business websites (up to 10)
  for phone numbers when not found in search snippets

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Track E: parseLiteralsSection compressed path position specs — complete unified pos_gt (#779)

* feat: Prove parseLiteralsSection_pos_gt for all literal types

Add position advancement proofs for the compressed/treeless literals
path (types 2-3), complementing existing raw/RLE specs (types 0-1).
Compose both into a unified parseLiteralsSection_pos_gt theorem.

Key changes:
- Remove `private` from parseCompressedLiteralsHeader to enable
  proof reasoning about header byte counts (≥3)
- Add parseCompressedLiteralsHeader_headerBytes_ge helper lemma
- Add parseLiteralsSection_pos_gt_compressed for types 2-3
- Add parseLiteralsSection_pos_gt for all types (0-3)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* doc: add progress entry for session 6626fe38

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: document private function opacity and tuple projection patterns

Add two patterns to lean-monad-proofs skill:
1. Private functions are opaque cross-module — check visibility before
   writing proofs that need internal reasoning
2. Use destructured arguments in helper lemmas to avoid unreduced
   tuple projections after subst
